heading: Antietam Battlefield site; acquisition of lands, buildings, structures, and other property
label: 430nn Antietam Battlefield site; acquisition of lands, buildings, structures, and other property
text: The Secretary of the Interior is authorized, in his discretion, to acquire in behalf of the United States, through donations or by purchase at prices deemed by him reasonable or by condemnation in accordance with , lands, buildings, structures, and other property, or interests therein, which he may determine to be of historical interest in connection with the Antietam Battlefield site, the title to such property or interests to be satisfactory to the Secretary of the Interior: , That payment for such property or interests shall be made solely from donated funds. All such property and interests shall be a part of the Antietam Battlefield site and shall be subject to all laws and regulations applicable thereto.section 3113 of title 40Provided
notes: Codification“” substituted in text for “the Act of ()” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works.Section 3113 of title 4025 Stat. 357Pub. L. 107–217, § 5(c)116 Stat. 1303August 1, 1888Aug. 21, 2002

Antietam National Battlefield Site Redesignated as Antietam National Battlefield; Boundary Revision, , , as amended by , Oct, 25, 1988, , provided that:Pub. L. 95–625, title III, § 319(b)92 Stat. 3488Pub. L. 100–528, § 1(c)102 Stat. 2649Nov. 10, 1978“The Antietam National Battlefield Site established pursuant to such Act of [section 430 of this title] is hereby redesignated the ‘Antietam National Battlefield’. The boundaries of such battlefield are hereby revised to include the area generally depicted on the map referenced in subsection (a) of this section [set out as a note under section 430 of this title], which shall be on file and available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, Department of the Interior.”April 22, 1960oooo

The boundaries of such battlefield are hereby revised to include the area generally depicted on the map referenced in subsection (a) of this section [set out as a note under section 430 of this title], which shall be on file and available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, Department of the Interior.”April 22, 1960oooo
